I EXECUTIVE SUMMARY 
I.1 Products covered by this document 
This document covers the Copernicus Climate Change Service (C3S) sea ice thickness climate data record 
(CDR) v4.0, which is brokered to the Copernicus Marine Service.  
Product Name: SEAICE_GLO_PHY_CLIMATE_L3_MY_011_013: Monthly gridded sea ice thickness from 
multi-sensor satellite radar altimeter data in the northern hemisphere between October and April with 
a resolution of 25 km for the period 1995 to 2024. 
I.2 Summary of the results 
This data product is redistributed, therefore the full product quality documentation , including the 
Product Quality Assurance Document (Hendricks, 2024a) and the Product Quality Assessment Report 
(Hendricks, 2024b), are available at the C3S Climate Data Store (CDS)1. The present document provides 
a top-level summary of these reports.  
The quality of the monthly gridded sea ice thickness SEAICE_GLO_PHY_CLIMATE_L3_MY_011_013 from 
1995/10 to 2024/04 has been assessed by comparison with in-situ observations and with orbit 
crossovers.  
Sea Ice Thickness: Product quality indicators (bias, uncertainty and precision) vary considerably between 
the radar altimeter types (pulse-limited: ERS-22 & Envisat3 and synthetic aperture: CryoSat-24 & Sentinel-
3A/B5). The mean bias is -0.59 m for pulse-limited altimeters  and -0.13 m for synthetic aperture 
altimeters. T he uncertainty is 0.69 m for pulse-limited altimeters and 0.54 m for synthetic aperture 
altimeters. The mean absolute difference of low-latency (< 1 day) orbit crossovers shows a precision of 
0.53 m for ERS-2, 0.37m for Envisat, 0.33 m for CryoSat-2, 0.33m for Sentinel-3A and 0.32 m for Sentinel-
3B at 25 km scale.  
Important note: Sea ice thickness is underestimated considerably (0.5 m) with r

catalog_entry.summary: This document describes the quality and validation of the SEAICE_GLO_PHY_CLIMATE_L3_MY_011_013 product, a monthly gridded sea ice thickness dataset from multi-sensor satellite radar altimeter data in the northern hemisphere for the period 1995 to 2024.
